summary: Move internal types to com.mongodb.internal and exclude them from Javadoc
DBCollection should not return null WriteResult on an unacknowledged write
description:
In 2.x, an unacknowledged write operation on DBCollection returns a WriteResult, and any access to the WriteResult properties will trigger a call to the getlasterror command on the same socket, which is likely to fail with an exception.  

In 3.x we will no longer call getlasterror, and instead DBCollection returns a null WriteResult for any unacknowledged writes.  This ticket proposes to change DBCollection to be closer to 2.x: return a WriteResult, but throw an exception from all property accessors.

summary: Resolve difference between database/collection options classes in sync and async APIs
description:
async uses OperationOptions
sync uses MongoDatabaseOptions/MongoCollectionOptions

no good reason.

summary: MongoDatabase#getCollectionNames should return a MongoIterable
description: Since that's where the listCollections command is going.

summary: Refactor GetDatabaseNamesOperation
description: Rename to {{listDatabases}} and return a {{BatchCursor<T>}} any legacy methods can map the results to get a list of names.  The new api can be updated to provide both {{listDatabases}} and {{listDatabaseNames}} which is inline with other admin list apis ({{listCollections}}, {{listIndexes}})

summary: Ensure Async cursors can be cancelled
description:
Currently the way to stop iterating an async cursor is to return false from the Block.  

A better way might be to instead call cancel() on the Future returned from the forEach method() in the higher level API.  This models more closely the way a Subscription works in RxJava.

summary: 2.12.x breaks compatibility with mongoDB v1.8
description:
Using the Java driver 2.12.x against a mongoDB version 1.8 fails at startup due to com.mongodb.MongoTimeoutException caused by java.lang.NullPointerException (see stack trace below).

The root cause seems to be the expected existence of "versionArray" in com.mongodb.ServerStateNotifier.getVersion(final CommandResult buildInfoResult) (v. 2.12.0, later moved to ServerMonitor.getVersion in 2.12.2)

Apparently, mongoDB v1.8 doesn't provide the version as array.

Is having a structured ServerVersion class really worth breaking backwards compatibility? Looks rather accidental to me.

com.mongodb.MongoTimeoutException: Timed out after 10000 ms while waiting to connect. Client view of cluster state is {type=Unknown, servers=[{address=127.0.0.1:27017, type=Unknown, state=Connecting, exception={java.lang.NullPointerException}}]

summary: BsonDocumentCodec should be Collectible
description:
Currently BsonDocumentCodec does not implement CollectibleCodec.

It can't now because CollectibleCodec currently lives in the driver module.  We either need to bring that down into bson or else create a new codec in the driver module that wraps the one in bson.

summary: List parameters should be generalized
description: The Java driver function {{DBCollection.aggregate}} takes in a {{List<DBObject>}}, which has the consequence that you'll get a compiler error if you pass in a {{List<BasicDBObject>}} instead.  This issue could be eliminated by changing the function to take {{List<? extends DBObject>}} instead.  It appears that similar issues exist for {{DBCollection.explainAggregate}} and {{DBCollection.insert}}.

summary: Document that MongoClient no longer throws UnknownHostException
description:
http://grepcode.com/file/repo1.maven.org/maven2/org.mongodb/mongo-java-driver/2.12.1/com/mongodb/ServerAddress.java#ServerAddress


This code does not throw an UnknownHostException like older versions. There is no attempt to resolve an ip address of the host using the java library InetAddress.getByName like in 2.11 versions.

summary: Remove checkstyle exclude of com.mongo.async.*
description: I tried to remove it but it complains of Javadoc problems, so needs a bit more investigation.

summary: ListCollectionsOperation filters namespaces incorrectly
description: com.mongodb.operation.ListCollectionsOperation.FilteringBatchCursor and com.mongodb.operation.ListCollectionsOperation.FilteringAsyncBatchCursor report incorrect results due to the filtering behavior.  In the case where an entire batch is filtered out, hasNext can return true when there are no more results, and next can return an empty batch.

summary: DBCursor getters should not return copies for mutable objects
Update all 2.8 server references to 3.0.
Raise default value of MongoClientOptions.minHeartbeatFrequency
description: Testing has confirmed that the 10ms default value can cause too much load in realistic failover scenarios when there are numerous MongoClient instances connected to the same replica set.

summary: ServerAddressSelector returns a List with a null element
description:
On Jenkins, we occasionally see this stack trace:

{noformat}
java.lang.NullPointerException
	at com.mongodb.BaseCluster.getRandomServer(BaseCluster.java:204)
	at com.mongodb.BaseCluster.getServer(BaseCluster.java:75)
	at com.mongodb.DBTCPConnector.getServer(DBTCPConnector.java:650)
	at com.mongodb.DBTCPConnector.access$300(DBTCPConnector.java:39)
	at com.mongodb.DBTCPConnector$MyPort.error(DBTCPConnector.java:482)
	at com.mongodb.DBTCPConnector.innerCall(DBTCPConnector.java:301)
	at com.mongodb.DBTCPConnector.call(DBTCPConnector.java:268)
	at com.mongodb.DBTCPConnectorTest.testConnectionReservationForReadThenWrite(DBTCPConnectorTest.java:137)
{noformat}

I've traced it down to ServerAddressSelector.choose returning a list with a single null element in the case where the ServerAddress is no longer in the cluster.

summary: Authentication can initially fail when connecting to a replica set when seed list host names do not match replica set host names
description:
This bug was introduced recently in 2.13 when the driver added support for dynamically determining which authentication mechanism to use when none is provided.  Consider the following sequence of events:

* Replica set created with host names a, b, c
* MongoClient created with seed list a1, b1, c1
* Application thread executes an query against a secondary
* Application thread waits for a secondary
* Monitor thread notifies that a1 is secondary
* Application thread selects the a1 server
* Application thread gets a connection to a1
* Monitor thread notifies that b1 is primary, and hosts list is actually a, b, c.  So a1, b1, c1 are removed from the cluster description
* Application thread tries to get a1's server description from the cluster description, assuming that it must still be there, but as it's been removed, it gets null.

Result is:

{noformat} 
java.lang.NullPointerException
	at com.mongodb.DBPort.authenticate(DBPort.java:367)
	at com.mongodb.DBPort.checkAuth(DBPort.java:402)
	at com.mongodb.DBTCPConnector.innerCall(DBTCPConnector.java:289)
	at com.mongodb.DBTCPConnector.call(DBTCPConnector.java:269)
{noformat} 

*Workaround:*

Use the same host names in the MongoClient seed list as are used in the replica set configuration

summary: Distinct show follow convention and return a MongoIterable rather than a list
description: Also allows for the operation to be future compatible if distinct ever produces a cursor

summary: Migrate QuickTour and QuickTourAdmin
description: Migrate the QuickTour and QuickTourAdmin examples over to 3.0.0

summary: Add batchSize to the MongoIterable
description: Currently there is no simple way to set the {{batchSize}} before calling {{batchCursor}} on a {{MongoIterable}} in the async driver and the sync driver should mirror the api also.

summary: Make all MongoIterable<T> methods fluid
description: Review if a fluent interface would be preferable for: {{aggregate}}, {{distinct}} and {{mapReduce}}.

make data by c-driver

bson_append_int32( &bson_array, "", -1, 1 );
bson_append_int32( &bson_array, "", -1, 2 );
bson_append_array_end( &bson, &bson_array );

summary: Remove errant Deprecation annotation on MongoClientOptions.Builder.localThreshold()
description:
Copy and past error, this should never have been deprecated.

Thanks to https://github.com/cranphin for pointing this out.

summary: maxWaitTime documentation should specify valid range and behavior
description:
This page: http://api.mongodb.org/java/2.6/com/mongodb/MongoOptions.html#maxWaitTime

The documentation should say what the default is, and the range of valid values.

Empirically (and code seems to agree), the default is 120000, and a setting of zero means infinite.

summary: Update async listDatabaseNames to use BsonDocument
description: Currently uses Document and libraries wrapping the method need to have the DocumentCodecRegistry

summary: Java driver throws error with $query
Collectible codec implicitly expects documents to be mutable
description: Either we should be explicit or we should return {{T}} from {{generateIdIfAbsentFromDocument}}

summary: Improve changing codecs
description: Currently wrapping {{MongoClient}} and changing the codec repository is painful especially compared to {{MongoDatabase}}.

summary: GridFS#findOne(ObjectId) finds by "objectId" instead of "_id"
Add Bson interface to enable type-safe MongoCollection API
description:
Introduce the Bson interface, and replaced all use of Object in the high-level API with it.

BasicDBObject, Document, and BsonDocument will  implement this interface, so any of them can be used to specify filter, sort, projection, update, etc.  Third parties can create their own implementations to allow for other types to be used that are not included in the driver proper.

summary: Add maxTime property to findAnd*Options classes
Implement tests from server selection specification
description:
See:

* https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-selection/server-selection-tests.rst
* https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/tree/master/source/server-selection/tests

summary: Regression applying read preference with tag set
description:

I followed instructions given http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/tutorial/configure-replica-set-tag-sets/ and http://docs.mongodb.org/ecosystem/drivers/java-replica-set-semantics/

I have tag secondary:0 and secondary:1 - and want my clients to load-balance between those two secondary nodes when reading data (query + aggregations)

but I always get following error

{code}
com.mongodb.MongoTimeoutException: Timed out after 30000 ms while waiting for a server that matches ReadPreferenceServerSelector{readPreference=secondary: [TagSet{[Tag{name='secondary', value='0'}]}]}. Client view of cluster state is {type=REPLICA_SET, servers=[{address=host3:27019, type=REPLICA_SET_SECONDARY, TagSet{[Tag{name='test', value='BsonString{value='a'}'}, Tag{name='secondary', value='BsonString{value='0'}'}]}, roundTripTime=1.8 ms, state=CONNECTED}, {address=host2:27019, type=REPLICA_SET_SECONDARY, TagSet{[Tag{name='test', value='BsonString{value='b'}'}, Tag{name='secondary', value='BsonString{value='0'}'}]}, roundTripTime=1.8 ms, state=CONNECTED}, {address=host1:27019, type=REPLICA_SET_PRIMARY, TagSet{[Tag{name='secondary', value='BsonString{value='a'}'}]}, roundTripTime=1.7 ms, state=CONNECTED}]

summary: LazyBsonObject.entrySet/keySet doesn't handle null values
Link all 2.8 new features to manual
description:
Reminder ticket.

Any new features that are released in 2.8 eg: {{mongodb.server.release 2.8}} will need links to the mongodb docs site.

For example any operations: ListIndexOperation, CreateIndexesOperation, ListCollectionOperation etc..

com.mongodb,version=[3.0,4]
description:
Hi, I downloaded the mongo-java-driver-3.0.0-beta3 and tried to created the osgi bundle . when i deployed the bundle in my AEM instance it is failing with the below error 
Imported packages :com.mongodb,version=[3.0,4) -- Cannot be resolved

Please close this if it is not a bug and let me know how to fix this.

Thanks
Muthu

summary: Comply with index management specification
SSL connections do not verify certificate hostnames
Add parse methods to Document class
description:
To match BsonDocument.parse.

There should be an overload that takes a DocumentCodec, as with toJson

summary: Empty objects and arrays not handled correctly in results.
Update to Netty 4.0.26.FINAL
description: Currently we're pegged to 4.0.24.FINAL.

summary: API documentation change for class DBCollection
description:
In the Javadoc for class DBCollection there is a mistake.

It is written for an example on how to use the class:
"MongoClient mongoClient = newMongoClient(newServerAddress("localhost", 27017));
DB db = mongo.getDB("mydb");
DBCollection collection = db.getCollection("test"); "

BUT when initialising DB it should say: 
"DB db = mongoClient.getDB("mydb");"

as the MongoClient variable name is "mongoClient" and not "mongo".

Link to Javadoc: name.http://api.mongodb.org/java/current/com/mongodb/DBCollection.html

summary: Broken link in api documentation
description:
Thought I would let you know that there is a broken link in the java driver:

https://api.mongodb.org/java/3.0/

Class: Enum JsonMode

Link text:  MongoDB extended JSON documentation.

Broken link:  

The requested URL /java/3.0/org/bson/json/docs.mongodb.org/manual/reference/mongodb-extended-json/ was not found on this server.

summary: JsonReader throws exception with some valid Shell Mode ISODate constructores
Remove closeInput from BsonBinaryReader and closeOutput from BsonBinaryWriter
description: The binary reader/writer classes should not be responsible for closing the input/output streams that they are constructed with.

summary: OSGi Import-Package is missing javax.crypto and javax.crypto.spec
description:
The SCRAM-SHA1 authentication mechanism requires two javax packages:

* javax.crypto
* javax.crypto.spec

but these are missing from the OSGi Import-Package in the MANIFEST.MF.
